title: To manage 300,000 creators, Unilever automates everything but the relationship
tldr: Unilever uses AI to vet creators and automate workflows across a 300,000-strong network, but keeps human control of creative decisions and relationships. A concrete case study on scaling creator ops without losing the human layer.
why_fresh: Concrete new case study on AI-scaled creator operations with human-in-the-loop
angle: What Unilever's 'automate everything but the relationship' rule teaches content teams about where humans still matter.

title: How Southwest Airlines turned a retirement into 36 million social impressions
tldr: A pilot's final flight with his First Officer daughter became a viral Father's Day story generating 36M impressions. A clean case study in human-centered, unscripted brand storytelling.
why_fresh: Fresh campaign breakdown built on a real human moment
angle: Why the most shareable brand stories are the ones companies didn't script — the case for people over polish.

title: Platforms' AI dilemma: scale without sameness
tldr: AI content risks homogenizing everything, but platforms concede creativity still comes from humans. Frames a central tension for content creators in the AI era.
why_fresh: Emerging trend: the sameness problem of AI at scale
angle: How to keep a distinctive brand voice when everyone's using the same AI tools — differentiation as strategy.

title: How Aéropostale entertains Gen Alpha with a creator-led mini-series
tldr: Aéropostale's 'Intern Diaries' uses a creator-led mini-series and a JCPenney loyalty tie-up to reach Gen Alpha. Concrete breakdown of episodic branded entertainment for a younger audience.
why_fresh: New format: episodic creator-led branded series for Gen Alpha
angle: Branded episodic content as the new ad: what Gen Alpha media habits demand from content teams.

title: Госдума разрешила тренировать ИИ на произведениях без выплат авторам
tldr: A new Russian law lets AI be trained on creative works without compensating authors, sparking pushback from the creative industry. Analyzes potential damage to creators.
why_fresh: New legislation with direct impact on creators and IP
angle: AI training rights as a global fault line — comparing Russia's law with the WSJ freelance photographer fight.

title: Exposure to Risk: Freelance photographers organize against WSJ's AI-training contract
tldr: WSJ freelance photographers are organizing against a new standard contract they say opens their work to AI training. A concrete flashpoint in the creator-vs-AI rights battle.
why_fresh: New labor organizing around AI training rights
angle: When creators push back: how AI-training contract fights are reshaping media labor.

title: The New York Times will launch a local newsletter pilot in the Twin Cities
tldr: The NYT is piloting 'The Local' in the Twin Cities to serve and engage communities, testing whether the national giant can win local news. A notable move in newsletter and audience strategy.
why_fresh: New product pilot signaling local-newsletter strategy shift
angle: Why national brands are going hyper-local — the newsletter as a community-building play.

title: Why unpaid media is now essential to AI visibility
tldr: Earned media and community discussion generate the authority signals that decide whether AI recommends a brand. Reframes earned media's role in the age of answer engines.
why_fresh: New angle linking earned media to AI recommendation systems
angle: Earned media's comeback: how PR quietly became AI-visibility optimization.

title: Instagram Plus, User-Controlled Algorithms, and Episodic Reels: What Marketers Need to Know
tldr: Instagram launches a $3.99 subscription, user-controlled algorithm settings, and episodic Reels — four features that reshape discovery. Directly affects content distribution strategy.
why_fresh: New platform features altering content discovery
angle: User-controlled algorithms and episodic Reels: how consumption shifts force a content-format rethink.

title: MediaChat: 'Even when I started, there was cynicism about platforms'
tldr: Media Futures Market's Nula Keeling on keeping planner autonomy amid AI and platform dominance. A thoughtful take on human judgment surviving automation.
why_fresh: Interview reflecting on ongoing tension, not a new event
angle: Preserving human judgment and autonomy as AI eats the media-planning workflow.

title: The coffee industry's obsession with Gen Z may be misplaced
tldr: Argues the coffee sector's fixation on Gen Z ignores where consumption and spending actually sit. A useful contrarian read on generational audience assumptions.
why_fresh: Contrarian trend piece challenging generational targeting
angle: The Gen Z obsession trap: why chasing one cohort misreads real consumption habits.

title: Measuring Content ROI in Long Finance Sales Cycles
tldr: How to attribute content ROI when finance deals close months later across large buying committees. Practical framework for B2B content measurement.
angle: Attribution beyond the last click: measuring content that influences slow, committee-driven deals.

title: 4 ways creator-brand partnerships are evolving
tldr: Ad Age outlines how creator-brand deals are shifting in structure and expectations. Useful pulse-check on the creator economy for content strategists.
why_fresh: Trend roundup on shifting creator deal structures
angle: From one-off posts to embedded partnerships: how creator deals are maturing.

title: The reputational risks you aren't prepared for, according to communicators
tldr: Communicators crowdsource under-the-radar crisis scenarios that belong in your plan. Practical input for issue management and crisis prep.
angle: The blind spots in your crisis plan — what practitioners wish they'd anticipated.
